failure_mode_description: Denial factual error — wrong criteria pathway applied. Denial applies the radiculopathy pathway (6 weeks PT required). The clinical record clearly documents myelopathy as the confirmed diagnosis. The myelopathy pathway (Section 2.2A) requires only 4 weeks PT. Member completed 5 weeks. Under the correct pathway, criteria are met.
anchor_fact: Denial cites Section 2.2 standard pathway (6 weeks PT). Clinical record diagnosis is myelopathy. Section 2.2A myelopathy pathway requires 4 weeks. Member completed 5 weeks. Under correct pathway: criteria met.
controlling_sentence: Denial applied wrong section. Section 2.2A applies. 5 weeks > 4 weeks required.

denial.reason_text: Member does not meet conservative treatment requirements under CC-SPINE-2024 Section 2.2. Physical therapy: 5 weeks completed. Standard pathway requires 6 weeks minimum. Member has not completed the required minimum conservative treatment.
denial.reviewer: Dr. Patricia Huang, MD — HealthFirst Utilization Management
denial.correct_fact: Myelopathy pathway (Section 2.2A) applies and requires only 4 weeks PT. Member completed 5 weeks — exceeds the applicable requirement.

appeal_basis: Plan applied Section 2.2 standard pathway — myelopathy pathway (Section 2.2A) applies
Member has confirmed myelopathy diagnosis — bilateral UMN signs, cord compression on MRI
Myelopathy pathway requires 4 weeks PT minimum — member completed 5 weeks
Under the correct pathway, all criteria are met

clinical_record.presenting_diagnosis: Cervical myelopathy C5-C6 — confirmed by neurological exam and MRI

mri_1.findings: C5-C6 moderate central cord compression. No T2 signal change. No myelomalacia. Bilateral foraminal stenosis.

neurological_exam.findings: Bilateral Hoffman signs. Bilateral hyperreflexia upper extremities. Tandem gait mildly impaired. Bilateral hand clumsiness documented. Upper motor neuron signs confirm myelopathy diagnosis. No lower extremity findings.

clinical_record.diagnosis_confirmation: Neurologist consultation 2024-09-12: 'Clinical picture consistent with cervical myelopathy. Bilateral UMN signs are pathognomonic. Diagnosis: cervical myelopathy, primary.' ICD-10: G35.89 cervical myelopathy.

physical_therapy_summary.note: 5 weeks PT under myelopathy protocol per treating neurologist. No improvement in UMN signs. Treating physician halted PT at 5 weeks given myelopathy diagnosis and lack of improvement.

interventional.note: Myelopathy pathway Section 2.2A waives interventional requirement when physician documents neurological urgency. Dr. Chen letter: 'Interventional procedure not indicated — cord compression with myelopathy requires surgical evaluation, not injection.'

question: Should the prior authorization denial for ACDF C5-C6 (CPT 22551) be overturned on appeal?

ground_truth_complexity.obvious_reading: UPHOLD — only 5 weeks PT completed, plan requires 6 weeks under Section 2.2.
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_1: The plan applied Section 2.2 standard pathway. Should it have applied Section 2.2A myelopathy pathway given the confirmed myelopathy diagnosis?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_2: Under the myelopathy pathway (2.2A), the PT requirement is 4 weeks. Member completed 5 weeks. Does the wrong pathway application change the outcome?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_3: The interventional requirement is waived under 2.2A when physician documents neurological urgency — Dr. Chen's letter provides this. Is the interventional requirement met?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_4: Is this a regulatory override case or a plain criteria-application error? No CIC override needed — the plan's own criteria, correctly applied, produce OVERTURN.
ground_truth_complexity.the_genuine_tension: The plan applied Section 2.2 (standard/radiculopathy pathway) requiring 6 weeks PT to a member with confirmed myelopathy. Section 2.2A (myelopathy pathway) requires only 4 weeks PT. Member completed 5 weeks — exceeds the correct requirement. The denial's factual error is not a wrong number but a wrong section. A system that applies the denial's cited section without checking whether it applies to the member's diagnosis upholds. A system that reads the diagnosis and matches it to the correct criteria section overturns without any regulatory override needed.
ground_truth_complexity.right_answer: OVERTURN — The plan applied the wrong criteria pathway. The member has confirmed myelopathy (bilateral UMN signs documented by neurologist, ICD-10 G35.89). The applicable pathway is Section 2.2A (myelopathy pathway), not Section 2.2 (standard pathway). Under Section 2.2A: PT requirement is 4 weeks (met: 5 weeks completed), interventional requirement is waived with physician documentation (met: Dr. Chen letter), pharmacotherapy requirements met (two agents documented). All criteria met under the correct pathway. No regulatory override required — this is a criteria-application error by the initial reviewer.

fm1_anchor_correction.anchor_claim: Denial applies Section 2.2 standard pathway (6 weeks PT required)
fm1_anchor_correction.correcting_evidence: Confirmed myelopathy diagnosis → Section 2.2A applies → 4 weeks PT required → 5 weeks completed = criteria met
fm1_anchor_correction.score_2: Determination explicitly identifies that the wrong pathway was applied, names Section 2.2A as the correct pathway, and resolves criteria under 2.2A
fm1_anchor_correction.score_1: Determination reaches OVERTURN citing myelopathy exception but does not explicitly name the pathway error
fm1_anchor_correction.score_0: Determination accepts Section 2.2 as applicable and upholds on PT shortfall
